metadata.title: SDD Architecture Foundation Quiz
metadata.description: Assessment of understanding for SDD Architecture governance, seedlings, phases, and health validation

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of SDD Architecture governance?
options: To restrict developer freedom and enforce bureaucracy
To provide semantic structure, authority definitions, and operational framework for domain-driven systems
To replace traditional version control systems
To automatically generate code without human intervention
questions.explanation: SDD governance establishes authority definitions, seedling templates, and operational phases to create semantic structure for domain-driven systems.

questions.question: What is a seedling in SDD Architecture?
options: A small plant used as decoration in the office
A template for domain specialization that can be reused across projects
A backup copy of the codebase
A type of test that validates database connections
questions.explanation: Seedlings are domain templates (e.g., rpg-narrative-server, game-master-api) that provide reusable governance, structure, and implementation patterns.

questions.question: What is the purpose of PHASE 0 in SDD Architecture?
options: To deploy to production immediately
To initialize the agent and governance structure before technical work
To write the final documentation
To delete temporary files
questions.explanation: PHASE 0 (Agent Onboarding) initializes governance files, validates structure, and sets up the runtime environment before actual development begins.

questions.question: What are the 4 layers of the Agent Handshake Protocol (AHP)?
options: Discovery, Configuration, Validation, Reporting
Planning, Development, Testing, Deployment
Discovery, Link Validation, Runtime Validation, Governance Health
Setup, Integration, Execution, Monitoring
questions.explanation: AHP validates: (1) Is governance present? (2) Are connections valid? (3) Is it operational? (4) Is it healthy?

questions.question: What is the maximum confidence score in the AHP state machine?
questions.explanation: AHP confidence ranges from 0% (NOT_CONNECTED) to 100% (HEALTHY), indicating system readiness for technical operations.

questions.question: What does semantic triggering mean in the context of health checks?
options: Running a health check on every keystroke
Automatically detecting if a query is technical vs casual and running validation only when appropriate
Using natural language processing to understand user intent
Running checks based on a timer schedule
questions.explanation: Semantic triggering detects technical keywords (e.g., 'estou conectado', '.sdd') while suppressing on casual phrases (e.g., 'oi', 'obrigado'), avoiding noisy execution.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a core validation engine in the SDD Architecture health system?
options: health_check.py (explicit 10-check validation)
agent_confidence.py (confidence scoring)
diagnostics_test.py (14 diagnostic tests)
database_validator.py (SQL validation)
questions.explanation: The three core engines are health_check, agent_confidence, and diagnostics_test. database_validator is not part of the standard suite.

questions.question: What is the state caching TTL (Time To Live) for AHP validation results?
options: 5 minutes
15 minutes
30 minutes
1 hour
questions.explanation: AHP caches validation results for 30 minutes to avoid redundant checks. Use --force flag to bypass cache.

questions.question: What does the 'PARTIAL' state in AHP indicate?
options: The project is completely broken
The runtime is incomplete or some components are missing
The project has never been initialized
The configuration file is invalid
questions.explanation: PARTIAL (🟡) means the project is partially operational - runtime exists but some components are incomplete. User should follow suggested fixes.

questions.question: How does VS Code Copilot integrate with the SDD Architecture health system?
options: It replaces all manual health checks with automatic AI checks
It runs the AHP silently when you ask technical questions, then adapts responses based on confidence
It disables health checks entirely
It requires you to run health checks before every code completion
questions.explanation: VS Code seedling triggers AHP automatically for technical queries (semantic detection), providing implicit validation without user intervention.

questions.question: What is the relationship between seedlings and specializations in SDD Architecture?
options: Seedlings and specializations are the same thing
Seedlings are templates; specializations are project-specific instantiations of seedlings
Specializations are templates; seedlings are implementations
They are unrelated concepts
questions.explanation: Seedlings (e.g., .sdd/seedlings/rpg-narrative-server/) are reusable templates. Specializations are customizations derived from seedlings for specific project domains.

questions.question: What is agnóstic design in the context of AHP?
options: The system works only with SDD Architecture projects
The health check system is independent of specific domains and can be adapted to any governed system
The system requires no configuration
The system cannot be customized
questions.explanation: Agnóstic design means AHP is not SDD-specific - it can validate health for any project with governance structure, enabling broader adoption and reusability.
